en.title: Transcribe YouTube Video — Extract, Copy & Download Transcripts Free
en.metaDescription: Extract YouTube transcripts instantly. Copy plain text or with timestamps, download as SRT, VTT, or TXT. Free, fast, no sign-up required. Works with 100+ languages.
en.heroSubtitle: Paste any YouTube video URL to extract the full transcript. Copy as plain text or with timestamps, download as SRT, VTT, or TXT — completely free, no sign-up required.
en.instantExtractionDesc: Get the full transcript in seconds — no sign-up required.
en.oneClickCopyDesc: Copy the entire transcript to your clipboard with a single click.
en.searchFilterDesc: Quickly find any word or phrase within the transcript.
en.howItWorksTitle: How to Transcribe a YouTube Video
en.howItWorksSubtitle: Get any video's transcript in three simple steps — no login, no software, no cost.
en.step1Desc: Find any YouTube video and copy its URL from the address bar or the share button.
en.step2Desc: Paste the URL into the input field above and click "Extract Transcript." We'll fetch the captions automatically.
en.step3Desc: Read, search, copy to clipboard, or download the transcript as SRT, VTT, or TXT — all for free.
en.featuresTitle: Everything You Need to Work with YouTube Transcripts
en.featuresSubtitle: A complete toolkit for extracting, reading, and exporting video transcripts.
en.freeExtractionDesc: Extract YouTube video transcripts at no cost. No account, no limits — just paste a URL and go.
en.langSupportDesc: Automatically detects the video's original language. Works with English, Spanish, French, Arabic, and 100+ more.
en.downloadFormats: Download as SRT, VTT, or TXT
en.downloadFormatsDesc: Export transcripts in the format you need: SRT for video editing, VTT for web, or TXT for notes.
en.timestampsDesc: Copy with or without timestamps. Each paragraph block shows the exact time position in the video.
en.builtInSearchDesc: Search through the transcript to find specific words, phrases, or topics instantly — with highlight matching.
en.autoCaptionsDesc: Extracts both creator-uploaded and YouTube auto-generated captions. Falls back intelligently when needed.
en.useCasesSubtitle: From students to marketers, transcripts unlock the value hidden inside video content.
en.studentsDesc: Turn lectures, tutorials, and educational videos into searchable study notes. Copy key sections and organize them for revision.
en.creatorsDesc: Repurpose video content into blog posts, social media captions, or newsletter copy. Save hours of manual transcription.
en.marketersDesc: Analyze competitor videos, extract key insights, and identify trends. Use transcripts to fuel content strategy.
en.accessibilityDesc: Make video content accessible to deaf and hard-of-hearing audiences. Generate captions for videos that lack them.
en.formatsSubtitle: Export your transcript in the format that fits your workflow.
en.srtDesc: The most widely used subtitle format. Compatible with Premiere Pro, Final Cut, DaVinci Resolve, VLC, and most video editors.
en.vttDesc: The web standard for video captions. Use with HTML5 video players, online courses, and web-based media platforms.
en.txtDesc: Clean, unformatted text for reading, note-taking, or pasting into documents, AI tools, and text editors.
en.langSectionSubtitle: Our tool automatically detects the original language of any YouTube video and fetches the correct transcript — no manual configuration needed.
en.langSectionNote: Supports both creator-uploaded captions and YouTube's auto-generated subtitles. The tool prioritizes the video's original language automatically.
en.faq1Q: Is this YouTube transcript tool free?
en.faq1A: Yes, completely free. There are no hidden fees, no account required, and no usage limits. Just paste a YouTube URL and extract the transcript instantly.
en.faq2A: Our tool automatically detects and extracts the original language of any YouTube video. It works with 100+ languages including English, Spanish, French, German, Portuguese, Arabic, Hindi, Japanese, Korean, Chinese, and many more.
en.faq3A: You can download transcripts in three formats: SRT (SubRip Subtitle) for video editing, VTT (WebVTT) for web players, and TXT (plain text) for notes and documents.
en.faq4A: Yes. You can copy the transcript with or without timestamps. The "Copy with timestamps" button includes MM:SS timestamps alongside each paragraph. Download formats like SRT and VTT also include precise timing data.
en.faq5Q: Can I extract transcripts from any YouTube video?
en.faq5A: You can extract transcripts from any public YouTube video that has captions enabled — either manually uploaded by the creator or auto-generated by YouTube. Videos with captions disabled by the creator cannot be transcribed.
en.faq6Q: How does auto-generated caption detection work?
en.faq6A: Our tool automatically checks for creator-uploaded captions first, then falls back to YouTube's auto-generated captions (ASR). It always prioritizes the video's original language to give you the most accurate transcript.
en.faq7Q: Is there a limit on video length or number of transcripts?
en.faq7A: No. There are no limits on video length or the number of transcripts you can extract. You can transcribe videos of any duration as many times as you need.
en.faq8Q: Do I need to install any software?
en.faq8A: No installation required. TranscribeYoutubeVideo runs entirely in your browser. Just visit the website, paste a YouTube URL, and get your transcript instantly.
en.ctaTitle: Ready to Extract Your First Transcript?
en.ctaSubtitle: Paste a YouTube URL above and get the full transcript in seconds.
en.footerTagline: Extract, copy, and download YouTube video transcripts — free and instant.
en.footerDisclaimer: transcribeyoutubevideo.com is not affiliated with YouTube. This tool is meant for educational purposes only.

fil.creatorsDesc: Repurpose video content into blog posts, social media captions, or newsletter copy. Save hours of manual transcription.
fil.marketersDesc: Analyze competitor videos, extract key insights, and identify trends. Use transcripts to fuel content strategy.
fil.accessibilityDesc: Make video content accessible to deaf and hard-of-hearing audiences. Generate captions for videos that lack them.
fil.formatsSubtitle: Export your transcript in the format that fits your workflow.
fil.srtDesc: The most widely used subtitle format. Compatible with Premiere Pro, Final Cut, DaVinci Resolve, VLC, and most video editors.
fil.vttDesc: The web standard for video captions. Use with HTML5 video players, online courses, and web-based media platforms.
fil.txtDesc: Clean, unformatted text for reading, note-taking, or pasting into documents, AI tools, and text editors.
fil.langSectionSubtitle: Our tool automatically detects the original language of any YouTube video and fetches the correct transcript — no manual configuration needed.
fil.langSectionNote: Supports both creator-uploaded captions and YouTube's auto-generated subtitles. The tool prioritizes the video's original language automatically.
fil.faq1Q: Is this YouTube transcript tool free?
fil.faq1A: Yes, completely free. There are no hidden fees, no account required, and no usage limits. Just paste a YouTube URL and extract the transcript instantly.
fil.faq2A: Our tool automatically detects and extracts the original language of any YouTube video. It works with 100+ languages including English, Spanish, French, German, Portuguese, Arabic, Hindi, Japanese, Korean, Chinese, and many more.
fil.faq3A: You can download transcripts in three formats: SRT (SubRip Subtitle) for video editing, VTT (WebVTT) for web players, and TXT (plain text) for notes and documents.
fil.faq4A: Yes. You can copy the transcript with or without timestamps. The "Copy with timestamps" button includes MM:SS timestamps alongside each paragraph. Download formats like SRT and VTT also include precise timing data.
fil.faq5Q: Can I extract transcripts from any YouTube video?
fil.faq5A: You can extract transcripts from any public YouTube video that has captions enabled — either manually uploaded by the creator or auto-generated by YouTube. Videos with captions disabled by the creator cannot be transcribed.
fil.faq6Q: How does auto-generated caption detection work?
fil.faq6A: Our tool automatically checks for creator-uploaded captions first, then falls back to YouTube's auto-generated captions (ASR). It always prioritizes the video's original language to give you the most accurate transcript.
fil.faq7Q: Is there a limit on video length or number of transcripts?
fil.faq7A: No. There are no limits on video length or the number of transcripts you can extract. You can transcribe videos of any duration as many times as you need.
fil.faq8Q: Do I need to install any software?
fil.faq8A: No installation required. TranscribeYoutubeVideo runs entirely in your browser. Just visit the website, paste a YouTube URL, and get your transcript instantly.
fil.ctaTitle: Handa ka na bang i-extract ang iyong unang transcript?
fil.ctaSubtitle: Paste a YouTube URL above and get the full transcript in seconds.
fil.footerTagline: Extract, copy, and download YouTube video transcripts — free and instant.
fil.footerDisclaimer: transcribeyoutubevideo.com is not affiliated with YouTube. This tool is meant for educational purposes only.
